subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #14 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Jersey Cares Coat Drive -
Thank you!
Thank you for donating new and gently used winter jackets.  We collected about eight over-stuffed jackets.  We will collect on Monday as well for those last minute donations.
Komitoms Music
On Wednesday we were fortunate to have the wonderful talents of the
Komitoms
.  The group introduced the children to different types of instruments and rhythms and had everyone smiling.  We hope to bring them back soon!
Five in a Row
For the month of December we are using books related to winter holidays.  Please feel free to share your family traditions with your child's teacher.
Winter Candle
A candle is shared among neighbors as they celebrate different beliefs and traditions.
Teacher Read Aloud
Potatoes at Turtle Rock
A family celebrates the first night of Hanukkah with a night hike around the farm.
Teacher Read Aloud
My First Kwanzaa
Learn about the seven principals celebrated during Kwanzaa.
Author Reading
T'was Nochebuena
Celebrate the night before Christmas with family and friends.
Spanish Teacher Reading the Story
Enjoying the Bustle
It is the season of bustle.  Decorations, visiting family and friends, parties, and extra shopping, cooking, and cleaning all get squeezed into already full days. The adults feel a bit more stressed—and your children feel it too.
A gentle reminder: what 
children appreciate most during this busy season is the time they spend with you working and laughing together.
Try to simplify your traditions to the ones that hold the most meaning for you and reflect the values and memories you hope to pass on. When you explain to your children why these traditions matter, you help them deepen their understanding of what it means to be part of a family and strengthen those traditions for the future. And don’t be afraid to make new ones too!
Community Activities
All month, 5-9pm,
Turtle Back Zoo
is open (and free) for families to stroll through the zoo with the winter lights.
This Sunday (December 7th 11:30am) at the CoLab is a special story time by local kindergarten teacher/author, Claire Sinclair.
Details here.
Next Saturday (December 13th 11am) Maplewood Library is hosting
Wilma and the Wingers
for a music and interactive story time.
Next Saturday (December 13th 3:30pm) is the Third Annual Lantern Walk at the Eagle Rock Reservation.  This event is free but requires a
registration
.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Art with Ms. Jenny
The children were inspired to create their own mittens, hats and scarves from left over paper.  Future fashion designers!

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  There is no bad weather just poor clothing.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water 
throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
